human_verification.notes: Reviewed the chemicals environmental compliance package for specialty boundary quality, source quality, chemicals-sector workflow realism, authority-review handoff discipline, and delivery-contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to operator-side chemicals environmental compliance, including hazardous waste, EPCRA, TRI, SPCC, and pretreatment readiness.
Authority sources cover RMP, hazardous-waste generators, EPCRA, TRI, SPCC, and pretreatment guidance with refresh and decay rules.
Evaluation scenarios cover inspection recovery, reporting readiness, authority-review handoff, low-confidence escalation, and boundary-safe refusal.
The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/chemicals-plastics-and-materials-manufacturing/environmental-compliance-chemicals-specialist/ to preserve the chemicals-sector overlay lane.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around process safety, product stewardship, regulator authority, and legal signoff.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated chemicals-sector environmental compliance overlay where process-safety engineering and legal signoff remain external.
The package cleanly integrates with permitting and authority-review specialists while preserving chemicals-specific environmental workflows.
